description: Fix all TypeScript compilation errors and build failures across the monorepo. Run pnpm turbo typecheck and pnpm turbo build, diagnose failures, and fix them.
steps: Step 1 - Run pnpm install to ensure all dependencies are resolved.
Step 2 - Run pnpm turbo typecheck 2>&1 | tail -200 to identify all TypeScript errors across packages and apps.
Step 3 - Fix TypeScript errors in packages/ first (they are build dependencies for apps). Common issues: missing type imports, TS6305 project reference mismatches, AI SDK v4/v5 type mismatches.
Step 4 - Fix TypeScript errors in apps/ backends and frontends. Routes may reference fastify.prisma without proper typing (now fixed by FEAT-001's declare module), but there may be other issues.
Step 5 - Run pnpm turbo build to confirm the full build succeeds. Fix any remaining build failures.
Step 6 - Run pnpm turbo test to confirm tests pass. Fix any test failures caused by type changes or missing mocks.
acceptance_criteria: pnpm turbo typecheck completes with zero errors
pnpm turbo build completes successfully for all packages and apps
pnpm turbo test passes all tests
verification: Run pnpm turbo typecheck from /projects/sandbox/Quant-Ecosystem and confirm exit code 0
Run pnpm turbo build from /projects/sandbox/Quant-Ecosystem and confirm exit code 0
Run pnpm turbo test from /projects/sandbox/Quant-Ecosystem and confirm exit code 0
findings: Fixed AI SDK v5 type mismatches (LanguageModelV1 vs V2, maxTokens -> maxOutputTokens, promptTokens -> inputTokens). Database package paths in app tsconfig.backend.json files needed to point to dist/ output rather than src/ to avoid Prisma client resolution issues. Next.js apps had conflicting pages/ and app/ routes that needed cleanup. @quant/realtime needed a client-safe export path to avoid bundling Node.js modules in Next.js client builds. Server-core auth enforcement needed to include /healthz and /readyz in public paths. Tests updated to reflect auth enforcement behavior on unknown routes.
